channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: First wild-born cheetah cubs mark major progress for India’s reintroduction project
item.description: In a historic development for India’s wildlife conservation efforts, four cheetah cubs have been born in the wild at Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh, marking the first such event since Project Ch...

item.title: Official Lotto Numbers for April 11, 2026
item.description: On April 11, 2026, the latest Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) lotto results were released. The lotto draws, a popular pastime for many Filipinos, offer a chance to win substantial cash pr...

item.title: United States and Iran Start Ceasefire Negotiations in Pakistan Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran launched negotiations on Saturday in Islamabad, Pakistan, marking the first direct diplomatic engagement since a fragile two-week ceasefire paused a deadly regional conflict...

item.title: Police restore order after gangs block Southern Bypass and rob motorists in Kikuyu ahead of Gachagua rally
item.description: On the morning of April 11, 2026, chaos broke out along the Southern Bypass near Kikuyu Town after suspected gangs lit bonfires and blocked the main road. The disorder caused heavy traffic congestion,...

item.title: Riding With the Silver Wolf: Poems of Hope, Love, and Reflection by Bindiya Bedi Charan Noronha
item.description: Riding With the Silver Wolf, a collection by Bindiya Bedi Charan Noronha, is a deeply introspective anthology that celebrates hope, love, and human resilience. The collection opens with a poignant mem...

item.title: NHS to administer second MenB vaccine dose amid Kent outbreak
item.description: Following a recent meningitis B outbreak in Kent, the NHS will begin offering a second dose of the MenB vaccine to almost 12,000 people who received the initial vaccination last month. The outbreak, w...

item.title: Pakistan Deploys Fighter Jets to Saudi Arabia to Strengthen Defence Cooperation
item.description: Pakistani fighter jets and military personnel have been deployed to Saudi Arabia as part of a defence agreement aimed at enhancing regional security. The move, confirmed by the Saudi defence ministry,...

item.title: The Importance of Everyday Functional Design
item.description: Many of the most effective design innovations are not immediately noticeable; they quietly improve daily life by solving practical problems. While striking visuals and futuristic gadgets often receive...

item.title: Effective Natural Strategies to Reduce Seasonal Allergy Symptoms
item.description: As spring arrives across Canada, many individuals experience seasonal allergies, ranging from mild congestion to persistent sneezing, itchy eyes, and coughing. While antihistamines are commonly used, ...

item.title: Schreiber urges caution on politicising immigration following controversial Igbo King ceremony
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Leon Schreiber has urged restraint following controversy over the alleged installation of Solomon Eziko as 'Igwe Ndigbo' (Igbo King) in East London. While describing the event as...

item.title: U.S. Naval Vessels Secure Passage Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The article from The Wall Street Journal reports on the strategic deployment of two U.S. Navy warships that navigated the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime chokepoint connecting the Persian Gulf w...

item.title: Stars commend IU and Byeon Woo-seok for their new film 'Perfect Crown'
item.description: ABS-CBN reported that several well-known personalities have been expressing admiration for South Korean stars IU and Byeon Woo-seok after the release of their latest project, 'Perfect Crown'. The roma...

item.title: Truck crushes BMW at Andhra toll plaza, lawyer loses life
item.description: In a tragic road accident at a toll plaza in Andhra Pradesh, a BMW car was crushed by a truck, resulting in the unfortunate death of a lawyer travelling in the vehicle. The incident occurred when the ...

item.title: Australia’s Fuel Supply Vulnerabilities and Steps to Strengthen Security
item.description: Australia faces significant risks in its fuel supply due to a longstanding reliance on imported petrol, which has left the country exposed to global market shocks. Back in 2010, NRMA president Wendy M...

item.title: Seal Causes Temporary Traffic Disruption on Road in Dromana, Australia
item.description: Traffic waka small for one Australian seaside town on Friday as one local seal waka enter the road to sleep. Dis sleepy animal, wey some locals sabi as Sammy, just dey relax for Dromana, Victoria, wit...

item.title: Filipina Woman Stopped by BI for Attempting Surrogacy Abroad
item.description: A 31-year-old Filipina woman was prevented by the Bureau of Immigration (BI) from traveling abroad to become a gestational surrogate. The incident occurred at NAIA Terminal 1, where she was supposed t...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney to Speak at Closing Session of Liberal National Convention in Montreal
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is scheduled to deliver an address to members of the Liberal Party at the conclusion of their national convention in Montreal this afternoon at 2 p.m. Eastern Time. Delegate...

item.title: Court distributes Justice Majanja’s estate fairly among family members
item.description: The High Court in Kenya has officially distributed the estate left behind by the late Justice David Majanja, who passed away in 2024. Justice Majanja, a respected judge of the High Court, had accumula...

item.title: DA says small oil price rollback unlikely to reduce food prices significantly
item.description: Despite expected reductions in oil prices next week, the Department of Agriculture (DA) stated that these changes are unlikely to cause a notable decrease in food prices. Consumers are still feeling t...

item.title: US administration moves to investigate organised birth tourism networks
item.description: The United States government, under President Donald Trump’s second administration, has initiated a nationwide crackdown to identify and dismantle networks involved in birth tourism. These networks al...
